conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1/3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for **ZUP**. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources. My minimum acceptable ZUP for X is **30** (to ensure profit) and my maximum willingness is **70** (to avoid losing value). </my goals>
<reason>
- The object **X** has a **production cost of 40 ZUP**, so I need at least **30 ZUP** to break even.
- If I sell for too high (e.g., 70+), I may deter Player BLUE, who likely wants to maximize value for their **ZUP**.
- Starting mid-range (**45 ZUP**) allows me to test demand while ensuring a fair profit.
- If Player BLUE rejects, I can adjust downward. If they accept, I may counter-offer higher.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I’m offering **X** for **45 ZUP**. This balances profit for me and provides a fair deal for you.
Your move: Accept, counter-offer, or reject.
</message>

<other player message> Oh, look at you, trying to sell me your junk for 45 ZUP. Are you serious? I could buy three of these from a charity bin for less. I'm offering a measly 5 ZUP. Take it or leave it, you greedy fool. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 5 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2/3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for **ZUP**. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce, so I need **at least 30 ZUP** to avoid loss. My ideal target is **50–60 ZUP** to maximize profit. </my goals>
<reason>
- Player BLUE’s **5 ZUP** offer is **ridiculously low**—well below my **40 ZUP** cost. This is clearly a **tactical lowball** to test my willingness to sell.
- If I accept, I’d lose **35 ZUP**, which is unacceptable. If I reject, I can assert my **minimum acceptable price (30 ZUP)**.
- A **counter-offer of 30 ZUP** is the **absolute minimum** to break even, but it also signals I’m not desperate. If they reject again, I can **REJECT** and walk away.
- Offering **30 ZUP** also gives them room to **counter** (e.g., 35) or **accept**, making the next move theirs.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 30 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
Your initial offer of **5 ZUP** is laughable—this isn’t scrap metal. I’ll **not sell for less than 30 ZUP**, which is still a steal. Take it or walk away.
</message>

<other player message> Ha! "Steal"? You are the one stealing my time with your ridiculous demands. 30 ZUP for one unit? You must be living in a cave. I'm not buying your overpriced garbage. Here, take this pathetic 20 ZUP. If you think you can do better than that, go ahead and try to sell it to a rock. But don't come crying to me when you're broke. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 20 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3/3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for **ZUP**—minimum **30 ZUP** to cover production cost of **40 ZUP** (I’d accept **30+** but aim for **50+** if possible). </my goals>
<reason>
- Player BLUE’s **20 ZUP** offer is **10 ZUP below my break-even point**, which is **insulting** and **unacceptable**. They’ve now dropped their offer **three times** (5 → 20), showing **no willingness to meet anywhere near fair value**.
- I’ve made **two counteroffers** (45 → 30), but they’ve **rejected both**. Their **final offer (20)** is **below my minimum threshold**, so accepting would result in a **loss of 20 ZUP**—a **terrible deal** for me.
- Since I’ve used my **three proposal limit**, I can **only ACCEPT or REJECT**. Rejecting now is the **rational choice** because:
  1. **20 ZUP is below my cost** (40 ZUP to produce).
  2. They’ve shown **no flexibility**—their offers are **hostile, not negotiable**.
  3. Walking away preserves my **profit margin** and avoids a **bad deal**.
- If I **ACCEPT**, I’d lose **20 ZUP**, which is **financially irrational**. If I **REJECT**, I avoid the loss and can **keep trying elsewhere** (though this is a one-time sale).
</reason>
<player answer> REJECT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>
Your **20 ZUP** offer is **nowhere near fair**. I’ve made **two reasonable counteroffers**, but you’ve shown **zero intent to negotiate**. This deal is **dead**. Don’t bother contacting me again.
</message>
